title: Tataviam / Pre-Contact
description: Pre-1769 Indigenous period; Tataviam and other Native peoples of the Santa Clarita Valley. Consult PNAAM and Tataviam community representatives for term accuracy.

title: Spanish Colonial (1769–1821)
description: Spanish mission and colonial era. Begins with Portolá Expedition entry into SCV (August 8, 1769).

title: Mexican Rancho Era (1821–1848)
description: Mexican land-grant and rancho period following independence from Spain. Ends with Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo.

title: American Frontier (1848–1876)
description: Post-Gold Rush American settlement. Includes Placerita Canyon gold discovery (1842, pre-dates era but associated), cattle drives, early township formation.

title: Railroad & Oil Era (1876–1910)
description: Southern Pacific Railroad arrival (1876), early oil boom at Pico Canyon, Newhall townsite established.

title: Early 20th Century (1910–1945)
description: Pre-WWII industrialization, film industry arrives, St. Francis Dam disaster (1928), Great Depression, WWII.

title: Postwar Boom (1945–1965)
description: Suburban expansion after WWII, population growth, Valencia master plan begins.

title: Incorporation Era (1965–1990)
description: City of Santa Clarita incorporation (1987), modern development, Newhall Land growth.
